intro: Schizocalyx sterculioides, also known as African bitter bush, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its bitter, woody, and aromatic properties. Traditionally, Schizocalyx sterculioides has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, thanks to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine and modern pharmaceutical applications.
